next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) immediately to evaluate for endocardial vegetation and aortic involvement
Obtain computed tomography angiography (CTA) of the aorta to evaluate for mycotic aneurysm or progression of aortic pathology
Consult infectious disease specialist to guide management of Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia and duration of parenteral antibiotic therapy
Obtain serial blood cultures to monitor for clearance of bacteremia
Perform magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the thoracic spine to rule out spondylodiscitis or paraspinal abscess
Consider CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with dedicated aortic protocol to further characterize the mural thickening and fat stranding
Urgent transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to assess for vegetation and proximal aortic involvement
Repeat CTA of the chest and abdomen with aortic protocol to evaluate for mycotic aneurysm progression or aortic rupture
Immediate infectious disease consultation to manage Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia and initiate appropriate long-term intravenous antibiotic therapy
Cardiac surgical consultation for evaluation of suspected aortic catastrophe
Strict blood pressure control with intravenous agents to prevent further aortic insult
MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out spondylodiscitis or epidural abscess
Serial blood cultures to confirm clearance of bacteremia
